3D & Animation

PixyCAD is a native 3D CAD app for Mac and iPad, built for makers, designers and 3D-printing enthusiasts who want professional solid modelling without traditional CAD complexity. It combines an approachable modelling workflow with a professional geometry foundation powered by Parasolid. We are launching on Product Hunt to get feedback from people who care about CAD, 3D printing and better creative tools on Apple platforms.

Launch Team

Subscribe [...] Promoted

Andrea Sartori

📌

Hi Product Hunt — I’m Andrea, from PixyCAD team We built PixyCAD because many 3D CAD tools still feel too complex for makers and 3D-printing enthusiasts, especially when working on modern Apple devices. Mac and iPad users deserve a native modelling experience that feels approachable without giving up a professional solid-modelling foundation. PixyCAD is a native CAD app for Mac and iPad, powered by Parasolid. It supports workflows from sketching to solid modelling, with Apple Pencil support on iPad and export formats including STEP, Parasolid, STL and 3MF. PixyCAD is free to try with the Starter plan, and today we are mainly looking for honest feedback. Thank you for taking a look. [...] Launch Team

# PixyCAD Alternatives

Exploring PixyCAD alternatives? Try Sketch for polished UI design, prototyping, and handoff; Spline for friendly, multi-user 3D on the web; Procreate for pencil-first illustration on iPad; Lunacy with built-in assets and AI; or Womp for easy, playful 3D.

Subscribe [...] ## Highest rated 3D modeling products

Highest rated

For web-centric 3D, Spline offers real-time collaborative modeling, animation, and interactive embeds suited to product sites and Apple platforms. Production pipelines lean on Unity for cross-platform builds, asset ecosystem, and XR workflows. Rapid asset creation favors Meshy, converting text/images to textured meshes with PBR exports. Together they cover interactive web scenes, deployable apps, and fast prototyping.

results.title: Meshy: Create stunning 3D models with AI | Product Hunt
results.content: even if you disregarded all other features and solely used 1 image at a time, slightly editing the image (I use magic eraser ios app and send it directly to Meshy on the mobile site often and the results are always superb or potentially superb) you will eventually get it exactly how you intended, if not better, and most of the time on the first upload, which, at that speed, is astonishing and unrivaled. Every feature works this way I am finding. For instance, text-to-3D, used solely without images to direct the output--same--if you don't get what you want in the first try, the output will at least inform and/or inspire the changes you might consider making to the text prompt and with Meshy you can can generate at the speed of these considerations--at the speed of inspiration. And when
